market_summary: US equities at record highs — S&P 500 7,580 (+0.22%), Dow first close above 51K, Nasdaq 26,973. 9th consecutive winning week for S&P driven by AI/semiconductor rally. Oil -17% for May on US-Iran ceasefire hopes. Gold $4,539 (+0.97%). BTC $73.1K, weakening into $7.5B options expiry with 6-day ETF outflow streak ($2.54B).

key_events_next_week.expected_impact: HIGH — 188K bpd increase already decided, risk of accelerated cut unwinding

overnight_risks: US-Iran ceasefire deal unsigned — 'decision soon'. Oil gap risk Monday: down on deal, spike on collapse.
Suspected mine found in Strait of Hormuz — any shipping incident = instant oil spike + gold bid.
BoJ rate hike expectations (two more 25bp priced to ~1.1%) — JPY strength could trigger carry trade unwind.
China PMI data due Monday — weak print drags Asian equities and commodities.
BTC $7.5B options expiry settlement may affect weekend/Monday spot. Max pain at $75K vs spot $73.1K.
PCE inflation at 3-year high — hawkish repricing risk if Fed speakers comment Monday.

instrument_news.XAG-USD: Silver $76, +3% for May. G/S ratio compressing toward 60:1. Solar demand robust (125 Moz forecast). BUT Longi announced copper substitution in back-contact solar cells — structural headwind. June seasonally weakest month. COT: specs reducing longs.
instrument_news.XAU-USD: Gold $4,539 (+0.97%). Central banks bought ~190t in Q1 2026. US gold ETFs $0.83B inflows in April. Iran deal binary risk dominates: deal = dip, no deal = bid. JPM expects 250t ETF inflows for 2026.
instrument_news.BTC-USD: BTC $73.1K. Spot ETFs 6 consecutive days outflows ($2.54B in 2 weeks). YTD ETF inflows just $536M. CFTC approved first US BTC perpetual futures May 29 (bullish long-term). Put/call 0.84 slightly bullish.
instrument_news.ETH-USD: ETH $2,004. Below max pain $2,200. Underperforming BTC. Hegota upgrade (Verkle Trees) scheduled H2 2026. $563M liquidations on May 18 selloff. Funding rates neutral-to-negative.
instrument_news.MSTR: MSTR $159.26 (+5%). Transferred 411 BTC to Coinbase Prime — sell speculation. 15 analysts avg Strong Buy, target $380.79 (+139%). Premium to NAV elevated but BTC-sensitive.
instrument_news.OIL: WTI $87.36 (-1.73%). Brent $91.37 (-1.43%). -17% for May on Iran ceasefire hopes. OPEC+ 188K bpd increase for June. 3.24M bpd cuts still in place. Suspected Hormuz mine = reversal risk.

metals_outlook: Gold at $4,539 with structural central bank bid (~190t/quarter). Iran deal outcome is dominant binary risk: deal = $4,400, collapse = $4,600+. Silver enters June seasonal weakness, COT specs reducing longs, but industrial demand (solar/EV) and G/S ratio compression provide medium-term support.
oil_outlook: WTI at $87 after brutal 17% May decline. June 7 OPEC+ meeting: 188K bpd locked in, signal of accelerated unwinding = $80. Iran deal collapse or Hormuz mine incident = snap to $95+. Market pricing peace.
crypto_outlook: BTC/ETH structurally fragile: $7.5B options expiry, 6-day ETF outflow streak, PCE inflation at 3-year high. NFP June 5 is the catalyst — strong jobs = hawkish repricing = more pressure; weak = bounce. BTC needs $75K reclaim to stabilize.
sentiment_shift: Risk-on in equities (9th S&P winning week, AI euphoria) but risk-off in crypto (ETF outflows) and commodities (oil crash) — divergence typically resolves within 2-3 weeks.
